home: Trinity Tree Psychological Services provides evidence-based care to promote growth and healing for children, teens, and adults. Our Services Therapeutic Services We offer Individual Therapy to nurture personal growth and emotional well-being, Couples and Family Therapy to deepen relationships and foster meaningful communication, and Child and Adolescent Therapy to support healthy emotional, behavioral, and social development. Discover Support Psychological Evaluations We offer comprehensive Psychodiagnostic and Neuropsychological Assessments for children, adolescents, and adults to evaluate cognitive, emotional, and behavioral functioning. Our evaluations identify conditions such as Autism, ADHD, anxiety, trauma, mood, and learning disorders, guiding personalized treatment and support. Explore Assessments Psychiatry and Medication We provide Psychiatric Care and Medication Management through licensed physicians and psychiatrists, offering expert evaluation, diagnosis, and treatment for children, adolescents, and adults. Our holistic approach integrates therapy and evidence-based medication management to support overall mental wellness. See Our Psychiatric Services Speech & Language Therapy We offer Speech Therapy services for children, adolescents, and adults to improve communication, language, and swallowing abilities. Our licensed Speech-Language Pathologists provide individualized care for speech, language, fluency, voice, and cognitive-communication needs. Learn More Softwave Tissue Regeneration Therapy (TRT) SoftWave® Therapy for Mind–Body Healing: Chronic pain doesn't just affect the body — it impacts mood, sleep, focus, and overall quality of life. At Trinity Tree Psychological Services, we recognize the deep connection between physical pain and emotional well-being. SoftWave® Tissue Regeneration Technologies (TRT) : helps reduce pain and activates the body's natural ability to heal. This cutting-edge, non-invasive therapy promotes tissue repair and pain relief without surgery or medication, supporting not only physical recovery but also emotional balance and improved daily functioning. Learn More We Are Here to Help! We are accepting new clients! 5422 West Thunderbird Road #5, Glendale, Arizona 85306, United States Phone: 623-444-5508 Fax: 623-444-5539 Email: Info@trinitytreepsych.com Our Hours Open today 08:00 am – 05:00 pm Reach Out for Support Reach Out for Support Name Email* Phone Attach Files Attachments (0) This site is protected by reCAPTCHA and the Google Privacy Policy and Terms of Service apply. about: About Our Story Some places treat mental health care like a checkbox. Trinity Tree was built to be something more. Since 2013, we have been creating a space where people feel genuinely seen, heard, and valued — where clinical excellence and authentic human connection are not competing priorities, but one and the same. What began as a psychological assessment practice has grown into something far greater: a multidisciplinary team uniting psychology, speech services, and psychiatric care under one roof, driven by a single, unwavering purpose. Trinity Tree was not built from a business plan. It was built from personal experience, deeply held values, and an unshakeable belief that every person — regardless of their story, their struggle, or where they are in life — deserves care that truly honors their dignity. Our Mission Mental health care should never feel out of reach. Our mission is to deliver exceptional, accessible care in an environment where people feel safe enough to be vulnerable, supported enough to grow, and respected enough to heal. We don't simply treat symptoms. We walk alongside the people we serve — through the hardest moments, the quiet victories, the breakthroughs, and everything in between. Our Values These aren't words on a wall. They are the standard we hold ourselves to every single day, in every room, with every person who walks through our doors. Integrity — We show up honestly, act ethically, and do right by every person in our care, even when no one is watching. Compassion — We lead with empathy. Every person who comes to us carries a story, and we handle it with the care and sensitivity it deserves. Respect — We honor the dignity, background, and individuality of every client, every time, without judgment or exception. Community — We are part of something far bigger than our practice. We are deeply invested in the well-being of the communities we are privileged to call home. Excellence — We hold ourselves to the highest clinical standards, because the people who place their trust in us deserve absolutely nothing less. Accessibility — Outstanding care means nothing if people cannot reach it. We actively work to dismantle every barrier that stands between people and the support they need. Our Promise At the heart of every appointment, every assessment, every conversation — there is a truth we never lose sight of: Your mental health matters. Not sometimes. Not for some people. Not when it's convenient. Always. For everyone. Without exception. Copyright © 2026 Trinity Tree Psychological Services - All Rights Reserved. Powered by This website uses cookies. therapeutic_services: Therapeutic Services Support Your Mental Wellness Today Personalized Therapy Personalized Therapy Personalized Therapy Trinity Tree provides individualized psychotherapy across the lifespan. Our licensed psychologists offer individual therapy for adults managing anxiety, depression, trauma, and life transitions; couples and family therapy targeting communication, conflict, and relational repair; and child and adolescent therapy addressing behavioral, emotional, and developmental concerns. All treatment is evidence-based, culturally responsive, and tailored to your specific goals. Couples & Family Personalized Therapy Personalized Therapy Trinity Tree provides evidence-based couples and family therapy delivered by licensed psychologists trained in relational and systemic approaches. Our couples therapy addresses communication breakdown, conflict patterns, intimacy concerns, and relational repair — helping partners develop the skills needed for a stronger, more connected relationship. Family therapy at Trinity Tree is strengths-based and collaborative, targeting communication, boundary-setting, and family system dynamics to foster a healthier, more supportive home environment. Child & Teen Support Personalized Therapy Child & Teen Support Trinity Tree provides developmentally informed psychotherapy for children and adolescents delivered by licensed psychologists with specialized training in youth mental health. Our clinicians utilize evidence-based modalities including Play Therapy, CBT, Trauma-Focused CBT, Behavioral Interventions, and Social Skills Training — selecting approaches appropriate to each child's developmental stage and presenting concerns. We specialize in the assessment and treatment of anxiety, depression, ADHD, behavioral challenges, trauma, and peer and social skills deficits. speech_language: Speech and Language Speech Therapy Services In addition to psychological care, we are proud to offer Speech Therapy services provided by our licensed Speech-Language Pathologist, supporting children, adolescents, and adults across the following areas: Articulation & Speech Sound Disorders — Improving the clarity and precision of speech sounds. Language Development — Addressing delays in understanding and using language effectively. Social Communication Skills — Supporting pragmatic language and social interaction, including for individuals with Autism Spectrum Disorder. Stuttering & Fluency Disorders — Helping reduce disruptions in speech flow for smoother, more confident communication. Voice Disorders — Improving vocal quality, pitch, and volume. Cognitive-Communication — Strengthening memory, problem-solving, and organizational skills. Swallowing Disorders (Dysphagia) — Assessment and treatment to support safe and effective swallowing. Comprehensive Evaluations & Assessments — Including diagnostic assessments for Autism Spectrum Disorder, speech and language delays, social communication challenges, and other communication-related disorders. These evaluations guide individualized therapy plans and identify strengths and areas for targeted support. Copyright © 2026 Trinity Tree Psychological Services - All Rights Reserved. psychiatry_medication: Psychiatry & Medication Psychiatry & Medication Psychiatric & Medication Care Our licensed psychiatrists and physicians (MD/DO) provide comprehensive psychiatric care and medication management for children, adolescents, and adults. Every patient is seen directly by a highly trained physician, ensuring expert evaluation, diagnosis, and ongoing treatment — no nurse practitioners. Medication Evaluation & Management — Thorough assessment of mental health concerns, individualized medication plans, and close monitoring of treatment effectiveness and side effects. Integration with Therapy — Psychiatric care is coordinated with therapy services for a holistic, personalized approach to overall well-being. Specialized Expertise — Our physicians bring extensive experience managing anxiety, depression, ADHD, trauma-related disorders, and other complex mental health conditions. Evidence-Based Approach — All medication recommendations follow current clinical guidelines and research to ensure safe, effective, and responsible treatment. Our psychiatrists provide expert evaluation, diagnosis, and medication management, working closely with therapy to support a holistic approach to mental health care. They specialize in treating: Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder (OCD) Personality Disorders Substance Use Disorders / Addiction Sleep Disorders Panic Disorder & Generalized Anxiety Disorder Eating Disorders Adjustment Disorders Patients at Trinity Tree Psychology receive direct access to physician-led psychiatric care, ongoing monitoring, and expert guidance tailored to their mental health needs. Parenting Help Empowering parents with tools to nurture healthy family bonds. Copyright © 2026 Trinity Tree Psychological Services - All Rights Reserved. Powered by This website uses cookies. psychological_evaluations: Psychological Evaluations Psycological Evaluations ASD & Social Skills Assessment Our assessments evaluate social communication, behavior patterns, and cognitive functioning to identify characteristics of Autism Spectrum Disorder. Using standardized tools and clinical observation, we provide a thorough understanding of each individual's strengths and challenges — helping guide tailored interventions and support for children, adolescents, and adults. Support for ADHD & Focus Challenges Testing examines attention, impulse control, working memory, and organizational skills to identify ADHD or executive functioning difficulties. Results inform targeted strategies to improve daily functioning and academic or occupational performance. Trauma & Adjustment Support Our assessments explore the psychological impact of trauma and significant life changes, identifying symptoms, coping abilities, and functional impairments to guide therapy and support meaningful recovery. Support for Learning Challenges Our evaluations assess cognitive functioning, memory, attention, problem-solving, and academic skills to identify learning disabilities and developmental challenges. Managing OCD & Anxiety Testing identifies obsessive-compulsive patterns, panic disorder, generalized anxiety, and related conditions, guiding treatment plans aimed at reducing distress and improving daily functioning. Personality & Mood Disorders Our evaluations assess patterns of thinking, feeling, and behavior, as well as emotional regulation — including conditions such as depression, anxiety, bipolar disorder, and personality disorders. These assessments identify symptom severity, functional impact, and underlying psychological patterns to guide personalized treatment plans and support long-term mental health. Managing Co-Occurring Challenges Our evaluations assess the impact of substance use on mental health and daily functioning, supporting integrated treatment planning, relapse prevention strategies, and recovery-focused interventions. Support for Eating Disorders Our evaluations identify patterns of disordered eating, body image concerns, and associated emotional and behavioral challenges, guiding treatment planning and providing strategies to support healthy nutrition, coping skills, and long-term recovery. Arizona Board of Nursing (AZBN) Evaluations Arizona Board of Nursing (AZBN) Evaluations - Comprehensive psychological assessments for nurses involved in Arizona Board of Nursing proceedings. Immigration Hardship Evaluations - Professional psychological assessments and documentation in support of immigration hardship waiver cases. neuropsychological_evals: Neuropsychological Evals Cognitive & Brain Assessments Brain Function Tests Personalized Guidance Brain Function Tests Our licensed psychologists conduct thorough assessments to help you understand your brain's health and how it influences your thoughts, feelings, and behaviors. Cognitive Profile Personalized Guidance Brain Function Tests We interpret your results to craft a detailed cognitive profile, providing clear reports and tailored recommendations for your mental health journey. Personalized Guidance Personalized Guidance Personalized Guidance We discuss your results thoroughly and provide personalized strategies to support your success at work, school, and home. Our Evaluation Steps At the start of every evaluation, we begin with an in-depth clinical interview to discuss your medical history, psychological background, and personal concerns. This collaborative conversation helps us gain a thorough understanding of your needs so we can design a personalized assessment plan. We then administer a tailored battery of tests to evaluate cognitive strengths and areas for growth. Some measures involve hands-on problem-solving and performance-based tasks, while others are self-report questionnaires capturing emotional, social, and behavioral functioning. The selection of assessments is guided by your age, background, and specific referral questions. Once testing is complete, we integrate the data to develop a clear profile of your cognitive and emotional functioning. We then meet with you to review the findings and provide practical, individualized recommendations to support success in everyday life. A detailed report is shared with your referring provider and other members of your care team, as appropriate. Our recommendations are tailored to each person and may include psychotherapy, family therapy, academic or occupational support, speech and language services, or other evidence-based interventions best suited to your needs. softwave_trt: Softwave TRT Healing with SoftWave TRT SoftWave® Tissue Regeneration Technologies (TRT) helps reduce pain and activates your body's natural ability to heal. This cutting-edge, non-invasive therapy has helped countless patients find lasting relief — without surgery or medication. Joint Pain Relief — Knees, hips, shoulders, elbows, ankles, wrists, and more. Soft Tissue Healing — Ligaments, tendons, muscles, and connective tissue. Chronic Pain Reduction — Back, neck, nerve, disc, and joint pain, as well as old injuries and post-surgical discomfort. A Safe, Non-Invasive Alternative — To surgery, cortisone injections, stem cell treatments, and long-term medication use. Acute Relief — For wounds, aches, numbness, and other localized pain. This patented technology targets injuries at the cellular level, stimulating your body's natural regenerative processes. By enhancing blood flow, activating connective tissue, reducing inflammation, and recruiting your body's own stem cells to the area, SoftWave® Therapy supports true healing from within. Many patients are able to avoid surgery, reduce reliance on pain medications, and experience natural, lasting relief — all without downtime or invasive procedures. Trusted & Proven for Recovery SoftWave®'s OrthoGold device is FDA 510(k) cleared for connective tissue activation, temporary reduction of minor aches and pains, improved blood supply, treatment of chronic diabetic foot ulcers, and superficial partial-thickness second-degree burns. This non-invasive treatment has earned the trust of top athletes and professional sports teams across the country, who have integrated SoftWave® into their recovery, rehabilitation, and performance routines. NFL — Pittsburgh Steelers, Miami Dolphins, Dallas Cowboys, Atlanta Falcons, New Orleans Saints NBA — Los Angeles Lakers, New York Knicks, Brooklyn Nets, Orlando Magic, Philadelphia 76ers MLB — Chicago Cubs, Atlanta Braves, Cleveland Guardians, Toronto Blue Jays Healing - Relief - Hope Supports Your Body’s Healing Supports Your Body’s Healing Supports Your Body’s Healing SoftWave® Therapy enhances your body's natural ability to heal, increasing circulation and tissue repair. Gentle Pain Relief Supports Your Body’s Healing Supports Your Body’s Healing Safe, needle-free, natural pain relief for joints, muscles, and nerves—no surgery needed. Trusted Outcomes Supports Your Body’s Healing Trusted Outcomes Join many others who have experienced reduced pain and regained mobility with SoftWave® Therapy. insurance_payments: Insurance & Payments Insurance & Payment Info Accepted Insurance We accept the following insurance plans for therapy and evaluations: Aetna Evernorth / Cigna UnitedHealthcare (UHC) Blue Cross Blue Shield Arizona (BCBS AZ) HealthNet Federal Services / Tricare If your insurance is not listed above, please see our other payment options below. Please note: SoftWave TRT is not SoftWave Therapyol SoftWave therapy is not covered by medicare, medicaid, or insurance at this time. We are hopeful this will change in the future given SoftWave’s FDA-clearance and clinical results. Patients can schedule a $79 New Patient Appointment , which includes an assessment and consultation along with a SoftWave treatment session. This allows patients to experience SoftWave and decide with the provider whether to continue with treatment. A full SoftWave treatment plan can range from 6 to 12 sessions, costing $150-per session. The number of sessions required will depend on the injuries and body’s response to therapy. Pay for Your Sessions Securely Clients may pay for services directly using personal funds. Payment can be made by cash, check, or credit/debit card at the time of the session. Use HSAs & FSAs for therapy costs These tax-advantaged accounts can be used to pay for eligible psychological services, with payment made directly from the account. Affordable Therapy Fees To make therapy more affordable, we offer a sliding scale fee structure based on income and family size. This ensures that services remain accessible and equitable for all clients. Copyright © 2026 Trinity Tree Psychological Services - All Rights Reserved. Powered by This website uses cookies. students: Students Practicum & Supervised Training Opportunities at Trinity Tree Contact Us Practicum Training Student Practicum & Training Opportunities Trinity Tree Psychological Services offers a structured practicum training experience for doctoral students in clinical or counseling psychology. Our practicum is rooted in compassion, evidence-based practice, and close clinical supervision — providing students with meaningful hands-on experience in a warm, multidisciplinary outpatient setting. About the Training Experience Practicum students train directly at our Glendale, Arizona outpatient clinic under the supervision of licensed psychologists. Training is competency-based and developmentally sequenced, meaning your level of clinical responsibility grows alongside your skills and confidence. What You Will Do Practicum students at Trinity Tree gain experience in three core areas: Psychological Assessment Students participate in structured intake interviews and psychodiagnostic evaluations across a range of presenting concerns. You will gain hands-on experience administering and interpreting standardized assessment tools, integrating findings, and contributing to comprehensive psychological reports. Assessment instruments include: • WAIS-5 — Wechsler Adult Intelligence Scale • WISC-5 — Wechsler Intelligence Scale for Children • WPPSI-IV — Wechsler Preschool and Primary Scale of Intelligence • WMS-5 — Wechsler Memory Scale, Fifth Edition • WIAT-4 — Wechsler Individual Achievement Test • ADOS-2 — Autism Diagnostic Observation Schedule • CARS-2 — Childhood Autism Rating Scale • Beery VMI — Beery-Buktenica Developmental Test of Visual-Motor Integration • Rey-15 — Rey 15-Item Memory Test • C-TONI-2 — Comprehensive Test of Nonverbal Intelligence • TRAILS A & B — Trail Making Test, Parts A & B • Trauma and suicide risk assessment measures • Conners 4 — Conners, Fourth Edition • VABS-3 — Vineland Adaptive Behavior Scales, Third Edition • Structured and semi-structured diagnostic interviews Intake Assessments Students conduct initial clinical interviews to gather relevant history, identify presenting concerns, and inform treatment planning. This experience builds foundational skills in differential diagnosis, clinical interviewing, and case conceptualization. Brief Psychotherapy Students provide short-term, evidence-based individual therapy under close supervision, working with children, adolescents, and adults across a variety of clinical presentations. Supervision All practicum students receive individual supervision from a licensed psychologist. Supervisors retain full legal and clinical responsibility for all services provided. You will never work without direct access to your supervisor. Who We Serve Our clients include children, adolescents, and adults presenting with a wide range of emotional, behavioral, and developmental concerns — giving you broad and diverse clinical exposure within a single training site. Contact For inquiries about practicum training opportunities, please reach out to us directly. General inquiries: info@trinitytreepsych.com Training Director: drshelton@trinitytreepsych.com Phone: (623) 444-5508 Address: 5422 W. Thunderbird Rd. #5, Glendale, AZ 85306 Website: www.trinitytreepsych.com APPIC Psychology Doctoral Internship Program Trinity Tree is currently in the process of developing a Psychology Doctoral Internship program with the goal of offering high-quality clinical training grounded in compassion, cultural humility, and evidence-based practice. Our aim is to create a supportive learning environment where emerging psychologists can grow, contribute meaningfully to the community, and develop the skills needed for competent and ethical practice. As part of this development process, we are making our program materials available for review. Prospective applicants, supervisors, and community partners are invited to explore our current Internship Brochure and Training Manual, which outline our training philosophy, structure, expected competencies, supervision model, and clinical opportunities. Resources & Info Brochure_2025_2026 (docx) Download Training_Manual_2025_2026 (docx) Download Copyright © 2026 Trinity Tree Psychological Services - All Rights Reserved. faqs: FAQ's FAQs Please reach us at if you cannot find an answer to your question. What are the therapists' qualifications? At Trinity Tree Psychological Services, we have several licensed clinical psychologists, post doctoral interns, and doctoral level students working toward advanced degrees in psychology. They also have extensive training and experience in various forms of therapy. What to Expect with Doctoral Students? When working with a doctoral level student as your therapist, you can expect a professional and beneficial therapeutic experience. However, there are some unique aspects to consider due to the student's status as a trainee. Here's what you can generally expect: 1) High-Quality Care: Doctoral level students are trained extensively in psychology and therapy techniques. They have a strong theoretical foundation and are up-to-date with the latest research and practices in the field. 2) Supervision: One of the most important aspects of therapy with a student is that doctoral students receive rigorous supervision from experienced licensed psychologists. This ensures that the therapy provided is evidence-based, ethical, and aligned with best practices. This supervision involves regular meetings where the student presents cases, treatment plans, and progress updates to their supervisor. 3) Collaboration: Supervised students often collaborate with their supervisors to ensure that their therapy is effective and appropriate for your needs. This collaboration enhances the quality of care you receive. 4) Cost-Effective: Therapy provided by supervised doctoral students is often more affordable than seeing a licensed psychologist. This can make mental health services more accessible for individuals who may not have the resources for full-price therapy. 5) Continuous Learning: Supervised students are in a learning phase, and they are motivated to provide the best possible care. They may consult with their supervisors to ensure they are using the most effective strategies for your situation. Issues We Treat Our therapists are trained to help clients with a wide range of issues including but not limited to depression, anxiety, relationship problems, trauma, ADHD, Autism, behavior concerns, and addiction. We also specialize in working with specific populations such as children, adolescents, and LGBTQ+ individuals. Are After-Hours Appointments Available? Our psychiatrists are accepting new clients for evening and weekend appointments. Therapy appointments however are typically limited to our business hours 8am-5pm on Mondays-Fridays. Child Speech Therapy Needs? If your child has trouble being understood, struggles to follow directions, stutters, or has difficulty expressing themselves, an evaluation can help determine if therapy is needed. When Can My Child Start Speech? Speech therapy can begin as soon as a delay or difficulty is identified. Early intervention often leads to the best outcomes. Support for Autism & ADHD Yes. We provide specialized support to help children with Autism Spectrum Disorder, ADHD, and other developmental needs improve communication and social interaction. Insurance We Accept Trinity Tree Psychological Services accepts the following insurance policies: Aetna, Evernorth/Cigna, UHC, BCBS AZ, and Healthnet Federal Services/Tricare. If your insurance is not listed please see what other types of payments are accepted below. Currently, we are unable to accept AHCCCS coverage. Other Payment Options Out-of-Pocket Payments: Clients can often pay for services directly using personal funds. This might involve payment by cash, check, or credit/debit card at the time of the session. Health Savings Accounts (HSAs) and Flexible Spending Accounts (FSAs): These are tax-advantaged accounts that individuals can use to pay for eligible medical expenses, including psychological services. Payments are made directly from the account. Sliding Scale Fees: This can make therapy more affordable for individuals who might not have insurance or who are experiencing financial hardship. Clients with lower annual incomes pay a lower fee per session, while those with higher incomes pay a higher fee. The fees are designed to reflect a percentage of the client's income, making it more equitable and affordable for everyone. Family size might also be taken into account, as larger families may have different financial needs. SoftWave Therapy Cost A full SoftWave treatment plan can range from 6 to 12 sessions, costing $150 per session. The number of sessions required will depend on the injuries and body’s response to therapy. To get started, patients can schedule a New Patient Appointment for $79 . This includes an assessment, consultation, and one SoftWave treatment session, allowing patients to experience SoftWave and decide whether to continue with treatment.
